meta.title: CBT Therapy This Week | Doctoral PhD/PsyD | Brooklyn Austin Miami
meta.description: CBT therapy with a doctoral therapist this week. PhD/PsyD only with the flexibility to flex into other methods when CBT isn't enough. $280-300/session. OON help.

hero.h1: CBT therapy this week, with a PhD or PsyD therapist who can flex if CBT alone isn't enough.
hero.subhead: First session as soon as this week. Every CBT therapist holds a PhD or PsyD, with CBT training built into doctoral curriculum, not a weekend course. And when CBT alone isn't getting at the root, your same therapist can flex into DBT, psychodynamic, or other evidence-based methods. No referrals out. No starting over.
hero.trustLine: ✓ First session this week ✓ $280 to $300 per session ✓ Out-of-network claim filed for you
hero.imageAlt: Clean, structured WTG office signaling evidence-based work

treat.h2: CBT is what most patients use here, and the rest of the team is here when CBT isn't enough
treat.subhead: Six ways our doctoral team supports adults using cognitive behavioral therapy and the modalities that pair with it. Every clinician PhD or PsyD.

cards.title: CBT for anxiety
cards.body: Structured cognitive behavioral therapy for generalized anxiety, panic, social anxiety, and health anxiety.

cards.title: CBT for depression
cards.body: Behavioral activation, cognitive restructuring, and the proven CBT framework for major and persistent depression.

cards.title: CBT for OCD and trauma
cards.body: CBT-based methods for OCD, trauma responses, and the conditions where CBT has the strongest evidence base.

cards.title: DBT for emotion regulation
cards.body: When emotion regulation, distress tolerance, or interpersonal effectiveness need a more structured framework alongside CBT.

cards.title: Psychodynamic and deeper work
cards.body: When CBT isn't getting at the root, your same doctoral therapist has training in deeper modalities.

cards.title: Psychiatry on the same team
cards.body: When medication can support CBT progress. One care plan, not two.

comparison.h2: How CBT therapy at WTG compares to the rest of the field
comparison.subhead: What you'll get from most options versus what happens at WTG.

rows.them: A CBT-only specialty practice that refers you out the moment CBT isn't the right tool.
rows.us: Doctoral therapists trained in CBT plus DBT, psychodynamic, and other approaches. Same therapist adapts to you, not the other way around.

rows.them: Master's-level therapist with a weekend CBT certification on top of a master's degree.
rows.us: Only doctoral clinicians, with CBT training built into the doctoral curriculum and supervised practice.

rows.them: You scroll a directory and pick a name based on a headshot and a paragraph bio.
rows.us: A real coordinator picks the CBT therapist whose style fits you (some are highly structured, others more conversational), on a five-minute call.

rows.them: Six-week waitlist before your first session.
rows.us: Most patients have their first CBT session the same week they call, match confirmed within 24 hours.

rows.them: Out-of-network paperwork dumped on you to figure out alone.
rows.us: Reimbursify files your OON claim for you, and most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps from your first call to your first CBT session
howItWorks.subhead: Under five minutes to start. No long forms, no chatbots, a real person answers and asks three questions about what you're working through.

steps.title: Call or tap to book
steps.body: Reach our patient coordinator by phone or online. A real person gets back to you the same day, on a five-minute call.

steps.title: Get matched in 24 hours
steps.body: We pair you with a doctoral CBT therapist for what you're working through, your schedule, and how structured you want the work. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

steps.title: First CBT session
steps.body: Meet in person or over secure video, often within the same week.

steps.title: Make progress
steps.body: Concrete goals, between-session work when it helps, and regular check-ins so you can see the change in your day-to-day.

whyWTG.h2: Five reasons CBT searchers choose WTG over a CBT-only specialty
whyWTG.subhead: Where doctoral CBT meets the flexibility CBT-only practices can't offer.

bullets.title: CBT plus the rest, with the same doctoral therapist
bullets.body: Specialty centers refer you out when CBT isn't the right tool. Your WTG therapist already has training in DBT, psychodynamic, and other evidence-based approaches. CBT when it works, something deeper when it doesn't, without changing therapists.

bullets.title: Doctoral-level CBT
bullets.body: Only PhD and PsyD clinicians, with CBT training built into the doctoral curriculum, not a weekend course.

bullets.title: Real coordinator match in 24 hours
bullets.body: Some CBT therapists are highly structured (homework, worksheets, between-session protocols). Others are more conversational. Your coordinator picks the style that fits how you actually like to work.

bullets.title: Psychiatry on the same team
bullets.body: When CBT works better with medication, our psychiatrists are on the same care plan as your therapist. No separate practice.

bullets.title: Same therapist, every session
bullets.body: We match you once. You don't restart with a new clinician every time someone leaves the practice.

clinicians.eyebrow: Meet a few of our therapists
clinicians.h2: Doctoral CBT specialists across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online
clinicians.subhead: Our coordinator can pair you with any doctoral therapist on the team trained in CBT. The four below are placeholder slots for real WTG CBT specialists; final names and headshots replace these before launch.

midCTA.body: Same-week first sessions for doctoral-level CBT across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online. The same therapist can flex into other methods when CBT isn't the full answer.

pricing.subhead: We're a cash-pay practice. No insurer dictating how many sessions you get, no diagnosis code filed to your permanent medical record.
pricing.reimbursement: Many patients have out-of-network benefits. We partner with Reimbursify, an independent service that submits your out-of-network claim to your insurer for you. Most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.
steps: We charge you the session fee at the time of service.
We send you a superbill you can submit to your insurance.
Reimbursify files the claim for you. You get reimbursed directly.

faq.h2: Everything CBT searchers ask before the first session

items.q: How much does CBT cost, and how long will it take?
items.a: Sessions are $280 to $300 each. CBT for anxiety or depression typically runs 12 to 20 sessions. Some patients need more for complex situations and others see meaningful progress in fewer. Your therapist will set goals with you in the first sessions and check progress regularly. We're not in the business of keeping you in therapy forever.

items.q: What's the difference between doctoral-level CBT and master's-level CBT?
items.a: Doctoral-level CBT means your therapist trained in CBT as part of their PhD or PsyD program, typically 4 to 7 years of doctoral training including supervised CBT practice. Master's-level CBT often comes from a weekend course or short certification on top of a master's degree. The methods overlap, but the depth of training, supervision, and case experience differs significantly.

items.q: What if CBT doesn't work for me?
items.a: It happens. Sometimes CBT isn't the right approach for what you're working through, and a CBT-only practice will refer you out to start over with a new therapist. Our doctoral therapists are trained in multiple evidence-based methods, so if CBT isn't getting at the root, your same therapist can shift to DBT, psychodynamic, or another approach. You don't change practices, you don't restart your story.

items.q: Do you offer CBT for OCD specifically (ERP)?
items.a: Some of our doctoral therapists are trained in CBT-based methods that include exposure-and-response work for OCD. Your coordinator can match you specifically with a clinician who has this experience. If ERP for OCD is the main reason you're reaching out, mention it during your first call so we pair you correctly.

items.q: Do you take my insurance for CBT?
items.a: We're a cash-pay practice, so we don't bill insurance directly. Most patients use out-of-network benefits and get reimbursed 50 to 80 percent of the session fee. We give you a superbill and partner with Reimbursify, a claim-filing service, that submits it to your insurer for you.

form.eyebrow: Ready to take the next step?
form.h2: Tell us a little about what you're looking for
form.subhead: Our patient coordinator will call or email you back the same day. No waitlists, no chatbots, no pressure. The first CBT session can be this week.
form.consent: By leaving your phone or email, you are consenting to receiving communications from us via text or email and that may include communications about your appointments, services, and marketing information related to our services. You may opt out of marketing information at any time. Your contact information will never be sold to any third party and will only be shared to the extent necessary to perform any services on your behalf.
form.phoneFallback: or call 786-692-6547 to speak with our coordinator now.
form.reassurance: Same-day response. No waitlists, no runaround.
